latest.title: Trust Capital Markets Event
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1350D
Trustpilot Group PLC
06 May 2026
Trustpilot Group Plc
Trust Capital Markets Event
Trustpilot Group Plc ("Trustpilot"
or the
"Company")
is hosting its Trust education event today for investors and sell-side analysts at its London headquarters.
The event will provide a comprehensive view of the governance, technological capability and commercial logic behind Trustpilot's position as a trust infrastructure platform. In a world where AI is making content effectively infinite, the event demonstrates why genuine human experiences are becoming more valuable, and how Trustpilot's compounding advantage in this area underpins our financial performance and competitive position.
Programme themes
The event will be led by Adrian Blair, CEO, and Shazadi Stinton, Chief Trust Officer, along with members of the wider Trust team. Key themes include:
●
Our compounding Trust advantage
- how the platform's integrity investment produces a self-reinforcing signal that grows more valuable as review volume and detection capability compound over time.
●
Structural independence
- how commercial relationships are structurally independent from content decisions, ensuring platform integrity cannot be influenced by payment status.
●
AI-powered fraud detection -
a proprietary system combining graph neural networks, weekly model retraining and human investigation, designed to outpace evolving fraud.
●
Openness as a competitive advantage -
why an open platform produces richer data than closed alternatives, and why this structural choice creates a strong moat.
●
Regulatory leadership -
how Trustpilot actively shapes global trust standards through proactive regulatory engagement ahead of legislation.
The Company is not disclosing any material new financial information or updating its current guidance.
Webcast
The main sessions will also be webcast live on the LSEG SparkLive platform from 2pm BST. Registration for the webcast is available via
this link
.
A replay of the webcast and associated presentation materials will be made available on the Trustpilot Investor Relations website.
Adrian Blair, CEO, commented:
"Trust is the foundation of our model. As AI makes content increasingly abundant, human experience becomes the scarce signal that people, businesses and AI systems depend on to navigate the world. Today we will demonstrate the discipline, governance and capabilities we've built. This is the foundation that gives us confidence in the financial targets we have set. "
Contacts
Trustpilot Group plc
Louise Bryant, Head of Investor Relations
Priyal Soni, Investor Relations
investor.relations@trustpilot.com
+44 (0) 7813 210 809
+44 (0) 7391 859 625
Headland Consultancy
Stephen Malthouse / Rob Walker / Charlie Pepper
trustpilot@headlandconsultancy.com
+44 (0) 20 3805 4822
About Trustpilot
Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever - to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial - we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.
Today, we have more than 361 million reviews and 160 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1,000 employees and we're headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Trustpilot runs a global review platform that lets customers rate and review businesses.  It also sells subscription services that give companies analytics, reputation management tools, and marketing insights based on those reviews.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the centre of online consumer decision‑making.  Millions of shoppers read Trustpilot reviews before buying, so the platform wields a lot of influence over retail and service sectors.  Its data‑analytics arm turns that traffic into a recurring revenue stream for businesses that want to understand and improve their customer experience.
company_30s.current_state: Trustpilot has just finished a strong 2025 earnings season, announced a share‑buyback programme, and brought on a new CFO.  The company is still trading at a steep discount to its pre‑crash valuation, but the latest moves suggest it is stabilising and may be positioning for a recovery.

rns_narrative: Corporate governance and capital‑market activity: new director appointments, a buy‑back declaration, and a transaction in the company’s own shares point to a focus on shareholder value.
Financial health and outlook: the 2025 annual report shows profitability ahead of expectations, and the upcoming AGM may reveal further buy‑back or dividend plans.
Market engagement: the company is hosting a trust‑education event and has a recent secondary placing, signalling efforts to broaden its investor base and strengthen market presence.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the solid 2025 results, the active buy‑back, and the new CFO’s track record.  Some investors see the education event as a sign that the brand is building a stronger community.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lingering volatility, the fact that the stock remains far below its all‑time high, and the risk of further dilution from share‑buyback activity.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has drifted from indifferent to slightly bullish as more positive company announcements surface, but engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, which means the market has largely ignored the stock and any news could generate a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The AGM on 16 April 2026 could confirm additional buy‑back or dividend plans, giving the stock a clear support level.
Q2 2026 earnings (likely in June) will test whether the profitability trend continues.
A new product launch or partnership with a major retailer could broaden the customer base and add a new revenue stream.
A sudden volume spike during any of the above events would signal renewed institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume surge coupled with a breakout above the 200‑day moving average on the daily chart.  A bullish engulfing or a short‑term reversal pattern would be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is still in the late phase of a deep crash; compression is tight, so a mean‑reversion is likely once a catalyst lifts sentiment.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen far below its historic peak, creating a steep upside window.  A bounce to the midpoint of the all‑time high would double the current price, and reaching the former peak would triple it.  The recent buy‑back and strong earnings give a solid foundation for a recovery.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Continued volatility could keep the price depressed, and further dilution from the buy‑back program or regulatory challenges could erode value.  The worst‑case scenario is a further 10‑15% drop from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200‑400% if the stock recovers to the midpoint or peak.  Potential downside: 70% of the historic peak.  The reward outweighs the risk by a factor of at least 3:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the recent resistance and volume exceeds the 3‑month average.  Use 10% of the portfolio, place a stop 20% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear volume spike at the 200‑day moving average or for the Q2 earnings release.  A breakout above the 200‑MA with at least 1.5× average daily volume would be the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Trustpilot’s deep crash, combined with a strong earnings beat and an active buy‑back, sets up a high‑asymmetry play: a 200‑400% upside is possible against a 70% downside risk if the company’s recovery catalyst materialises.
